name: Sun-Drenched Quiche
recipeInstructions:
For the pastry:
1. In a large mixing bowl, stir together flour and 1/2 teaspoon salt.
2. Cut in shortening and butter or margarine until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
3. Sprinkle on water, 1 tablespoon at a time, tossing with a fork until the pastry is moist enough to hold together.
4. Shape the dough into a ball, then flatten into a disk. Wrap in plastic wrap and refrigerate for 1 hour.
5. Roll the pastry between 2 sheets of waxed paper into an 11-inch circle. Ease the pastry into a 9-inch pie plate; trim and flute the edge.
For the filling:
6. In a small skillet, cook green onion in hot oil over medium heat about 1 minute. Spread the cooked onions in the bottom of the prepared crust.
7. Sprinkle mozzarella cheese, basil, parmesan cheese, and sun-dried tomatoes into the crust.
8. In a mixing bowl, whisk together eggs, half and half (or light cream), salt, pepper, ground red pepper, and nutmeg until combined.
9. Pour the egg mixture evenly over the filling in the crust.
10. Bake at 425°F for 15 minutes. Reduce the oven temperature to 375°F and bake about 25 more minutes or until the filling is set in the center.
11. Let stand for 5 to 10 minutes before cutting into wedges.
